title: mentha sylvestris var. royleana
intro: Mentha sylvestris var. royleana, also known as Royle's wild mint, is a variant of the wild mint plant, a perennial herb belonging to the Lamiaceae family. This plant is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly its essential oils, which contain menthol, menthone, and limonene, providing analgesic, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial effects. The health benefits of Royle's wild mint include relief from digestive issues, headaches, and respiratory problems, as well as its ability to reduce stress and promote better sleep. Royle's wild mint can be used in various herbal preparations, such as teas, infusions, and topical creams, making it a versatile natural remedy for various health concerns.
